    Contributing to spatialGE

    Thank you for your interest in contributing to spatialGE! This document provides guidelines for contributing to the package.

    Types of Contributions

    1. Report Bugs

    Report bugs at https://github.com/FridleyLab/spatialGE/issues

    When filing an issue, include: - Your operating system and R version - Steps to reproduce the bug - Expected vs actual behavior - A minimal reproducible example if possible

    2. Fix Bugs

    Look through the GitHub issues for bugs. Anything tagged with “bug” and “help wanted” is open to whoever wants to implement it.

    3. Implement Features

    Look through the GitHub issues for features. Anything tagged with “enhancement” and “help wanted” is open to whoever wants to implement it.

    4. Write Documentation

    You can never have enough documentation! Contributing to documentation includes: - Improving function examples in Roxygen comments - Writing vignettes for new workflows - Fixing typos in documentation - Adding FAQ entries

    5. Submit Feedback

    The best way to send feedback is to file an issue at https://github.com/FridleyLab/spatialGE/issues

    If you are proposing a feature: - Explain in detail how it would work - Keep the scope as narrow as possible - Remember that this is a volunteer-driven project

    Get Started!

    Ready to contribute? Here’s how to set up spatialGE for local development:

    1. Fork the spatialGE repo on GitHub

    2. Clone your fork locally:

      git clone git@github.com:your_name_here/spatialGE.git
cd spatialGE

    3. Install dependencies:

      install.packages("devtools")
devtools::install_deps()

    4. Create a branch for local development:

      git checkout -b name-of-your-bugfix-or-feature

    5. Make your changes following the guidelines below

    6. Test your changes:

      devtools::test()
devtools::check()

    7. Commit your changes and push your branch to GitHub:

      git add .
git commit -m "Your detailed description of your changes."
git push origin name-of-your-bugfix-or-feature

    8. Submit a pull request through the GitHub website

    Development Guidelines

    Code Style

    • Follow the tidyverse style guide
    • Use spaces, not tabs
    • Keep lines under 80 characters
    • Use meaningful variable names

    Documentation

    • All exported functions must have Roxygen documentation
    • Include @examples for every function
    • Examples should be <20 lines and use test data
    • Use \dontrun{} for examples that require internet or long runtime

    Testing

    • Write unit tests for new functions using testthat
    • Aim for >80% code coverage
    • All tests must pass before submitting a PR
    • Run devtools::test() before committing

    Commit Messages

    Follow Conventional Commits: - feat: - New features - fix: - Bug fixes - docs: - Documentation changes - refactor: - Code refactoring (no behavior change) - test: - Test additions or modifications - chore: - Maintenance tasks

    Example:

    feat: add spatial gradient detection to STgradient

- Added Spearman correlation for gradient detection
- Added log-distance transformation option
- Updated documentation with examples

    Pull Request Process

    1. Update the NEWS.md file with your changes
    2. Update documentation with devtools::document()
    3. Ensure all tests pass
    4. Request review from maintainers
    5. Address review comments
    6. Merge after approval

    Code of Conduct

    Please note that this project is released with a Code of Conduct. By participating in this project you agree to abide by its terms.

    Questions?

    Contact the maintainers: - Oscar Ospina: oscar.ospina@jhmi.edu - Alex Soupir: Alex.Soupir@moffitt.org

    Thank you for contributing to spatialGE! 🎉

    On this page

    Developed by Oscar Ospina, Alex Soupir, Brooke Fridley.
